stimulus: Former ocean explorer Dawn Wright says that although she doesn’t have a definite idea of when it might happen, she ______blank that humans will someday need to find ways to live sustainably beneath the surface of the sea. This guess informs her interest in future research missions to deep-ocean habitats.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale: Choice A is correct because "supposes" is a tentative guess, matching the text’s phrase "doesn’t have a definite idea" and "this guess." Choice B is incorrect because demanding is too forceful. Choice C is incorrect because doubting contradicts her belief that it may happen. Choice D is incorrect because establishing implies certainty.
